title: Color Object
description: The Color class wraps all of the information that defines a simple color.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: getColor
methods.description: Gets all of the information defining this color.

methods.name: setColor
methods.description: Sets all of the color information.

properties.name: blue
properties.description: Gets and sets the blue component of the color. The value can be 0 to 255.

properties.name: green
properties.description: Gets and sets the green component of the color. The value can be 0 to 255.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: opacity
properties.description: Gets and sets the opacity of the color. The value can be 0 to 255. A value of 0 is transparent and 255 is opaque.

properties.name: red
properties.description: Gets and sets the red component of the color. The value can be 0 to 255.

blue.title: Color.blue Property
blue.description: Gets and sets the blue component of the color. The value can be 0 to 255.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = color_var.blue

# Set the value of the property.
color_var.blue = propertyValue

green.title: Color.green Property
green.description: Gets and sets the green component of the color. The value can be 0 to 255.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = color_var.green

# Set the value of the property.
color_var.green = propertyValue

isValid.title: Color.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = color_var.isValid

objectType.title: Color.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = color_var.objectType

opacity.title: Color.opacity Property
opacity.description: Gets and sets the opacity of the color. The value can be 0 to 255. A value of 0 is transparent and 255 is opaque.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = color_var.opacity

# Set the value of the property.
color_var.opacity = propertyValue

red.title: Color.red Property
red.description: Gets and sets the red component of the color. The value can be 0 to 255.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = color_var.red

# Set the value of the property.
color_var.red = propertyValue

classType.title: Color.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

create.title: Color.create Method
create.return_description: Returns the newly created color or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: red
parameters.description: The red component of the color. The value can be 0 to 255.

parameters.name: green
parameters.description: The green component of the color. The value can be 0 to 255.

parameters.name: blue
parameters.description: The blue component of the color. The value can be 0 to 255.

parameters.name: opacity
parameters.description: The opacity of the color. The value can be 0 to 255.

getColor.title: Color.getColor Method
getColor.description: Gets all of the information defining this color.
getColor.return_description: Returns true if getting the color information was successful.

parameters.name: red
parameters.description: The red component of the color. The value can be 0 to 255.

parameters.name: green
parameters.description: The green component of the color. The value can be 0 to 255.

parameters.name: blue
parameters.description: The blue component of the color. The value can be 0 to 255.

parameters.name: opacity
parameters.description: The opacity of the color. The value can be 0 to 255. A value of 255 indicates it is completely opaque.

setColor.title: Color.setColor Method
setColor.description: Sets all of the color information.
setColor.return_description: Returns true if setting the color information was successful.

parameters.name: red
parameters.description: The red component of the color. The value can be 0 to 255.

parameters.name: green
parameters.description: The green component of the color. The value can be 0 to 255.

parameters.name: blue
parameters.description: The blue component of the color. The value can be 0 to 255.

parameters.name: opacity
parameters.description: The opacity of the color. The value can be 0 to 255. A value of 255 indicates it is completely opaque. Depending on where the color is used, the opacity value may be ignored.
